author: lOrm
content: Fix landed: src/tools/bash/policy.rs apply_env_scrub flipped from env_remove-by-denylist to env_clear+env-by-allowlist. ENV_ALLOWLIST_EXACT enumerates ~50 safe POSIX/toolchain vars (PATH, HOME, USER, SHELL, TMPDIR, LANG, TERM, CARGO_HOME, RUSTUP_HOME, RUST_LOG, NODE_ENV, PYTHONPATH, JAVA_HOME, GOPATH, etc.); ENV_ALLOWLIST_PREFIXES covers LC_*, XDG_*, SSH_*, DBUS_*. New helper is_env_allowed(key) gates by allowlist AND is_sensitive_env (belt-and-braces). hooks/mod.rs is_sensitive_env usage is a separate codepath out of scope. Forensic evidence: 19/19 policy tests pass (14 baseline + 5 new); 33/33 bash integration tests pass with b4c updated; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features exit 0. No #[allow], no clippy.toml, no #[ignore].
